steps.n: This is the basic particle meaning 'for' or 'to'
Adding مْ changes the meaning to make it negative
Adding أَ at the beginning makes it into a question that expects the answer 'yes'

steps.m: to open, expand, or make spacious
steps.n: This is the basic concept of opening or expanding something
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: we open/expand (addressing Allah in أَلَمْ نَشْرَحْ)
steps.n: Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed vowels to show it's happening now. This is Allah speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'.
Added fatha to show how the letter is pronounced
Added كَ at the end to show who is being addressed. In this ayah, Allah is speaking directly to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
Added fatha and sukun to make it a noun meaning the actual thing (chest) rather than an action

steps.m: your chest (object of نَشْرَحْ - we expand)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show it belongs to 'you' (male). This chest is what's being expanded in the phrase 'Did We not expand for you your chest?'

steps.m: to place or remove a burden

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense verb

steps.m: we placed/removed (referring to removing وِزْرَكَ - your burden)
steps.n: Added نا to show 'we' did the action. In this ayah, it refers to Allah removing the burden from the Prophet ﷺ.

steps.m: and we placed/removed (referring to removing وِزْرَكَ - your burden)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement. This shows Allah mentioning another blessing given to the Prophet ﷺ.
This is a preposition that shows something moving away from something else. In this ayah, it's used with وَضَعْنَا (we removed) to show what was removed from.

steps.m: from you (object of وَضَعْنَا - we removed)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. This connects to وَضَعْنَا (we removed) to show what was removed from you.

steps.m: to carry or bear a burden

steps.n: Added kasra after first root letter and sukun after second to create the noun meaning 'burden' or 'sin' - something that weighs heavily on someone
Added a small line (fatha) to show this word is being acted upon

steps.m: your burden/sin (object of وَضَعْنَا - We removed)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is your burden. This burden is what Allah is removing in this ayah when He says وَضَعْنَا (We removed).
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect clauses and describe something mentioned before
Adding ال makes it more specific and definite, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
The ل gets a shadda and the initial alif changes to a waṣla (ٱ) for smoother pronunciation when connected to previous words
The final ي is written as ى with an alif mark (ٓ) in the Quran. This word connects to the verb أَنقَضَ (weighed down) to tell us what did the weighing down.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it weighed heavily (on ظَهْرَكَ - your back)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and sukun on first root letter to show causing something to happen. In this ayah, it describes how something was made to weigh heavily on the Prophet's back.

steps.m: to show up or come out

steps.n: Changed from a verb root meaning 'appear' to a noun meaning 'back' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: your back (being weighed down by أَنقَضَ)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show it's your back. This back is being weighed down by the verb أَنقَضَ (weighed down).

steps.m: to raise, lift up, or elevate something higher

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This is the basic past tense verb form.
Adding نَا changes 'he' to 'we' as the doers of the action. This verb is connected to ذِكْرَكَ (your mention/status) which is what was raised.

steps.m: and we raised/lifted up (ذِكْرَكَ - your mention/status)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with what happened before. This shows Allah mentioning another blessing given to the Prophet ﷺ.
Added fatha to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for you (masculine singular) (connected to رَفَعْنَا - we raised)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كَ (you) to show who the action is for. This word connects to رَفَعْنَا (we raised) to show who the raising was done for.
Changed from basic root 'to mention' into a noun 'mention' by adding vowel marks, and fatha at end shows it receives the action of 'We raised'

steps.m: your mention (object of رَفَعْنَا - We raised)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to you. This word is receiving the action of رَفَعْنَا (We raised).
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis and certainty to what follows. The shadda (ّ) makes the ن sound stronger to show extra emphasis.

steps.m: for indeed/then certainly (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: The فَ prefix shows this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous verses to show that after hardship comes ease.
This is a basic preposition that shows accompaniment or being together with something

steps.m: with/along with (connects العُسْرِ hardship with يُسْرًا ease)
steps.n: Added fatha vowels to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, it connects the word 'hardship' with 'ease', showing they come together.
This pattern makes it an adjective describing something
Changed the root into a noun by adding ُ (damma) on first letter and ْ (sukun) on middle letter. This pattern is used to turn an action or state into a thing you can point to.
Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show this hardship is paired with يُسْرًا (ease) that comes after it in the ayah
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to point to this specific hardship that Allah is talking about, not just any hardship
The ي at the start shows it's happening now
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on second letter to change it from an action to the concept itself
Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this word is the object of مَعَ (with) in the phrase 'with ease'

This is a particle that has no root. It's one of the most common Arabic words used to emphasize what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that there will definitely be ease (يُسْرًا) with hardship.
This is a connecting word that shows two things go together. In this ayah, it shows that hardship comes with ease.
This pattern makes it an adjective describing something
Added vowels to make it a noun meaning the state of being difficult

steps.m: the hardship (connected to يُسْرًا/ease that follows)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word after it. This hardship is mentioned in contrast with the ease (يُسْرًا) that follows it in the ayah.
The ي at the start shows it's happening now
Changed to a noun meaning 'ease' by adding damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter

steps.m: ease (as the object of مَعَ - with)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha (ًا) to show this word is the object of the preposition مَعَ (with) in the phrase 'with ease'

steps.m: when (followed by فَرَغْتَ - you have finished)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that means 'when' or 'if'. In this ayah, it introduces what should happen after finishing something.

steps.m: and when (followed by فَرَغْتَ - you have finished)
steps.n: The فَ prefix adds the meaning of 'and' or 'then' to connect this with what came before. Here it connects the instruction about what to do after finishing with previous instructions.

steps.m: to finish or become free from something
steps.n: This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to change 'he' to 'you', and added sukun before it. This word is connected to فَٱنصَبْ (so exert yourself) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to work hard, to toil, to labor
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding fathas creates the basic past tense verb form
Changed from 'he worked hard' to 'work hard!' by making it a command - added اِ at start and dropped the last vowel
Added sukun at end to show it's the end of the word and changed kasra to damma for proper command form
Added فَ at beginning to show this comes after finishing something else (فَرَغْتَ). The alif changes to a special form (ٱ) because it comes after فَ
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination
Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is the standard written form of this word
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to what came before it. The word points towards رَبِّكَ (your Lord) in this ayah.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion
steps.n: The root letters convey the basic meaning of nurturing or caring
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
The word رَبّ (Lord) gets a kasra (i sound) under the ب when something possesses it, like 'your Lord'
The root conveys the basic meaning of wanting or desiring something
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he turned' to 'you turn!' by adding اِ at start and putting a stop mark (sukun) at end - this makes it a command

steps.m: so turn eagerly! (command to Prophet ﷺ regarding رَبِّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect with previous verse, showing this is the result or what should be done next. The command is specifically directed to the Prophet ﷺ about turning eagerly to his Lord.
